name: Curried Smoked Chicken and Wild Rice Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch scallions including the green part, chopped (about 3/4 cup)
6 whole smoked boneless chicken breasts (about, skinned 3 1/2 to 4 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
Make the dressing: In a blender or food processor blend the garlic, the vinegar, the lemon juice, the curry powder, the chutney, salt and pepper to taste until the mixture is smooth. With the motor running add the oil in a stream, then add the sour cream and the water, adding additional water if necessary to reach the desired consistency. Blend the dressing until it is combined well. Stir in the coriander.
Make the wild rice mixture: In a large heavy saucepan combine the wild rice with the water and the salt. Simmer it, covered, for 45 to 50 minutes, or until absorbed. Transfer the wild rice to a bowl and add the vinegar, oil, and salt and pepper to taste. Toss the mixture well and let cool completely. Stir in the scallions and raisins.
Cut the chicken into 3/4-inch pieces. Spoon the wild rice mixture onto one side of a large shallow serving dish, arrange the chicken on the other half, and decorate with mache down the middle. Serve the dressing separately. Alternatively, toss together in a large serving bowl and garnish with mache.
Yield: 12 servings
